roles.title: Staff Data Scientist (Growth & Marketing)
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft and domain match—the role heavily emphasizes causal inference, A/B testing, and marketing measurement, directly leveraging your core expertise. Staff-level positioning with thought-leader responsibilities aligns well with your player-coach preference. However, limited public comp data and unclear growth-stage maturity (appears Series C+, less 0-to-1 than preferred) are concerns; AI/ML relevance is modest unless the role expands into product ML, which isn't evident in the posting.
roles.description:
### **Why Charlie Health?**



Millions of people across the country are navigating mental health conditions, substance use disorders, and eating disorders, but too often, they're met with barriers to care. From limited local options and long wait times to treatment that lacks personalization, behavioral healthcare can leave people feeling unseen and unsupported.



Charlie Health exists to change that. Our mission is to connect the world to life\-saving behavioral health treatment. We deliver personalized, virtual care rooted in connection—between clients and clinicians, care teams, loved ones, and the communities that support them. By focusing on people with complex needs, we're expanding access to meaningful care and driving better outcomes from the comfort of home.



As a rapidly growing organization, we're reaching more communities every day and building a team that's redefining what behavioral health treatment can look like. If you're ready to use your skills to drive lasting change and help more people access the care they deserve, we'd love to meet you.

### **About the Role**



As a Staff Data Scientist \- Growth and Marketing, you'll be seen as a thought leader among Growth, Marketing, and Revenue partners to help them be rigorous and thoughtful on how to best use data. You will bring an experienced perspective among the team to inform their roadmaps, shape their thinking, and drive outcomes. You will contribute directly to the growth of the company, via streamlining marketing measurement and approaches, leveraging statistical rigor via modelling \& causal inference to teams, and via identifying new opportunities for continued and sustained impact across the org.



We're a team of passionate, forward\-thinking professionals eager to take on the challenge of the mental health crisis and play a formative role in providing life\-saving solutions. If you're inspired by our mission and energized by the opportunity to increase access to mental healthcare and impact millions of lives in a profound way, apply today.


### **Responsibilities**


* Partner with Growth, Marketing, Revenue, and Operations to turn ambiguous business questions into clear analytical recommendations that influence strategy and decision\-making
* Build and iterate on lead, account, and ICP scoring models that help reps prioritize efforts and partner with Ops on data\-driven territory design
* Develop models to analyze successful high\-performer rep patterns to optimize onboarding and productivity
* Architect and maintain a trusted marketing measurement framework (e.g. MMM, incrementality testing \[geo tests, holdouts, lift studies]), \& align on consistent metric definitions (e.g. CAC, conversion, retention)
* Develop spend optimization \& channel allocation frameworks that account for saturation, diminishing returns, and the tradeoff between short\-term acquisition and long\-term outcomes
* Apply causal inference methods (DiD, synthetic controls, quasi\-experiments) to own channel\-level incrementality measurement, with clear decision rules \& guardrails for each approach
* Design and scale repeatable experimentation frameworks for channel, creative, and audience testing, increasing velocity without compromising rigor

### **Requirements**


* 7\+ years in data science/analytics roles, at least 3 of those years with Marketing, Sales, and/or Revenue teams
* Demonstrated ability to navigate an ambiguous data environment, with start\-up experience preferred related questions and provide approaches with appropriate statistical rigor to a wide variety of stakeholders
* Familiarity with CRMs (e.g. Salesforce), Marketing Platforms, and the metrics / terminology associated with them (CAC, Opps, etc.)
* Experience with modeling work that spans at least some of: MMM, LTV, Lead Scoring, Attribution Modeling, and Segmentation
* Strong technical proficiency: SQL, Python or R, modern ELT/ETL, OLAP databases (e.g., Snowflake), dbt, BI tools (e.g., Tableau, Hex), and experience setting up and scaling experimentation programs (A/B tests, causal inference, lift measurement)
* Excellent ability to communicate, collaborate, and influence business partners at all levels
* Bonus: Healthcare Experience, Start\-up Experience
* Passion for mission\-driven work in mental health or healthcare settings and a desire to apply your skills to improve outcomes
* Please note: candidates located within a 75\-minute commute of our NYC office are expected to work onsite 4 days/week

### **Benefits**

  


Charlie Health is pleased to offer comprehensive benefits to all full\-time, exempt employees. Read more about our benefits here.


*Additional Information*


*The total target base compensation for this role will be between $190,000 and $270,000 per year at the commencement of employment. Please note, pay will be determined on an individualized basis and will be impacted by location, experience, expertise, internal pay equity, and other relevant business consid

roles.title: Senior Data Scientist, ASE iCloud Data Organization
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft alignment—the role heavily emphasizes experimentation, causal inference, and A/B testing at scale, directly matching core expertise. However, Apple is a mature mega-cap (not growth-stage Series B-D), which misaligns with the candidate's stated preference for 0-to-1 building and builder culture. Compensation likely falls short of $230-260K target at the Senior DS level (levels.fyi shows wide variance; senior roles typically $180-280K, but Apple's bureaucracy and legacy structure may cap this). The role offers technical depth and impact but in a large, complex organization rather than the nimble, high-ownership environment the candidate seeks.
roles.description:
Would you like to help shape the future of Apple’s products and platforms while impacting some of the most widely used software in the world? The Apple Services Engineering (ASE) organization drives innovation behind Apple’s cloud\-based experiences for over a billion global users \- including iCloud\+, Photos, Mail, Messages, FaceTime, Calendar, and Notes. We build the infrastructure and intelligence that enable Apple’s ecosystem to deliver seamless, privacy\-first experiences every day.  

  

The ASE Data Organization plays a central role in this mission by embedding data\-informed thinking into every decision. Partnering with product, engineering, and business teams, we combine analytical rigor, product acumen, and technical depth to generate insights, measure impact, and guide strategy across iCloud and related services.  

  

**Description**  

  

We are seeking an experienced Senior Data Scientist to drive high\-impact analytics for iCloud and Apple’s cloud services. You will collaborate across engineering, product, and business teams to uncover opportunities, optimize user experiences, and influence decisions at global scale.  

  

This role calls for a balance of technical depth, strategic thinking, and strong communication. You’ll deliver insights that shape the trajectory of Apple Services, lead complex analytical projects, and expand a culture of data\-informed decision\-making across ASE.","responsibilities":"Partner with Product, Engineering, Design, and Business leads to define priorities, shape measurement frameworks, and translate insights into growth strategies for iCloud.  

  

Deliver data\-informed narratives for senior leadership that directly inform product and business roadmaps.  

  

Drive organizational alignment by clarifying analytical outcomes and implications for diverse stakeholders.  

  

Design performance metrics that capture product health, user engagement, and business impact across iCloud services.  

  

Lead controlled experiments, apply causal frameworks and marketing mix models to evaluate initiatives at scale.  

  

Conduct deep behavioral analysis to uncover engagement, retention, and churn drivers, then translate findings into actionable strategies.  

  

Collaborate with Data Engineering to define instrumentation and build reliable data pipelines across ASE.  

  

Develop and productionize ML and statistical models that enable predictive and diagnostic insights for product decisions.  

  

Leverage generative AI to automate analytical workflows and expand measurement capabilities at scale.  

  

**Preferred Qualifications**  

  

Advanced degree in a quantitative field (Statistics, Mathematics, Computer Science, Engineering, Economics, or related discipline).  

  

Practical experience with experimentation (A/B testing, Multi\-armed bandit) and advanced causal inference methods (e.g., matched markets, synthetic controls).  

  

Hands\-on experience with large\-scale data ecosystems (Hive, Spark, Presto, etc.) and building scalable, reproducible analytical pipelines.  

  

Demonstrated success influencing or leading projects within matrixed, cross\-functional environments.  

  

**Minimum Qualifications**  

  

7\+ years of experience in data analytics or data science, with 5\+ years in data science applying statistical modeling, or Machine Learning in production.  

  

Proven ability to influence cross\-functional initiatives and shape product or business strategy using data\-informed insights.  

  

Strong communicator skilled at framing complex technical concepts for senior and executive audiences.  

  

Deep expertise in product analytics, launch reporting, and impact measurement.  

  

Proficiency in SQL and at least one programming language (Python, R, etc.), with strong statistical and experimental design foundations.  

  

Ability to structure ambiguous problem spaces, prioritize effectively, and deliver actionable insights.  

  

Exposure to applying generative AI or ML\-based agents (ex. Claude) to automate analytical workflows and build data products.  

  

Apple is an equal opportunity employer that is committed to inclusion and diversity. We seek to promote equal opportunity for all applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, Veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics. Learn more about your EEO rights as an applicant .  

  

**Pay \& Benefits**  

  

At Apple, base pay is one part of our total compensation package and is determined within a range. This provides the opportunity to progress as you grow and develop within a role. The base pay range for this role is between $171,600 and $302,200, and your base pay will depend on your skills, qualifications, experience, and location.  

  

Apple employees also have the opportunity to become an Apple shareholder through participation in Apple's discretionary employee stock programs. Appl

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist
roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and seniority match: the role explicitly demands experimentation leadership, causal inference, A/B testing, and mentorship—core strengths. Toast's restaurant/SMB focus aligns with consumer-marketplace domain preference and avoids blacklisted sectors. Primary concerns: (1) role appears to require internal Toast tenure as stated in posting ('technical leader for core data science at Toast'), which may signal internal promotion focus; (2) stage unclear—Toast is likely Series C+ (post-IPO trajectory) not Series B-D growth stage, and comp guidance unavailable; (3) AI/ML relevance present but not primary (menu rec, demand forecasting are classical ML). Still a high-quality fit for Staff-level scope and direct product impact.

roles.title: Director of Data Science, Trust & Safety, Signals and Content Understanding
roles.location: San Francisco, CA, US; Remote, US
roles.fit_notes: Strong seniority and compensation fit for a Director-level role at a mature public company with clear AI investment. However, this is a poor stage match—Pinterest is post-IPO (Series E+), not the Series B-D growth-stage startup the candidate prefers. The Trust & Safety domain is tangential to core experimentation/causal inference work; while metrics and signals are relevant, the role emphasizes policy enforcement and harm prevention rather than 0-to-1 product experimentation. Cultural fit is uncertain; large-scale orgs often lack the builder-driven intimacy candidates like this value.

roles.title: Head of Data Science - Monetization
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft match on experimentation and causal inference in a monetization context with clear player-coach leadership expectations. However, this is a public company (NYSE: NXDR) with mature infrastructure, not true 0-to-1 growth-stage building—better suited for scaling existing systems than greenfield platform development. Limited AI/ML product relevance unless you drive LLM applications in ads targeting or recommendations, which isn't evident in the posting. Domain fit is excellent (local marketplace/SMB monetization, not ads-tech per se, though ad-adjacent).

roles.title: Senior Data Scientist, Marketing
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft match on core expertise (MMM, causal inference, A/B testing, experimentation design) and solid domain fit (productivity/consumer, not ads/defense). However, this is a marketing analytics role at a mature, public company—not 0-to-1 building—and Dropbox is past the Series B-D growth stage the candidate targets. Compensation appears below target ($52K base per levels.fyi is clearly incomplete data, but Dropbox's mature-stage positioning suggests total comp may cluster $180-220K), and the role is IC-heavy with less player-coach/leadership stretch than ideal.

roles.title: Principal Product Analytics Engineer
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional craft and seniority alignment: this is a Principal-level, hands-on architecture role that directly leverages your experimentation platform and causal inference expertise to build product analytics infrastructure at scale. Strong domain fit (restaurant/SMB SaaS, not ads/defense), with clear player-coach responsibilities (architecture + partnership with analysts/PMs). Stage and culture signals are solid but less certain due to research limitations; Toast appears Series C+ (likely $1B+ valuation), which is late-stage for your growth preference, though the 0-to-1 analytics platform building partially offset this. AI relevance is present but secondary to data architecture work.

roles.title: Senior Product Analyst
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft alignment: Toast's emphasis on experimentation, A/B testing, measurement frameworks, and statistical modeling directly matches the candidate's core expertise in experimentation platforms and causal inference. Excellent domain fit for a restaurant/SMB SaaS company with no exposure to avoided sectors. Primary concerns: role is titled 'Senior Product Analyst' (IC-focused) rather than explicit player-coach/Staff level, and Toast appears to be later-stage (likely Series D+, post-IPO trajectory) rather than the preferred Series B-D growth stage where 0-to-1 building dominates. AI relevance is moderate—mentioned as 'utilizing AI tooling' but not positioned as LLM/ML product development.

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist, Marketplace Optimization and Ranking
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional craft and seniority alignment: this role is a textbook Staff-level player-coach position deeply rooted in experimentation, causal inference, and ranking systems—precisely the candidate's core expertise. Compensation is strong ($241K floor aligns well with $230-260K target). However, Airbnb is a mature, public company (Series E+), not the growth-stage 0-to-1 builder environment the candidate explicitly prefers. The role is also SF-based, requiring relocation rather than remote/NYC hybrid flexibility. Strong domain fit (marketplace, not ads/defense/crypto), solid AI relevance (search, personalization, LLM-adjacent), and top-tier team quality, but misaligned on company maturity and location.

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist, Engagement Ecosystem
roles.location: San Francisco, CA, US; Remote, US
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft and seniority match—Staff role with deep experimentation/causal inference work in engagement ecosystem directly aligns with core expertise. Compensation trajectory realistic ($230-260K achievable within Pinterest's band). Main concerns: Pinterest is mature/public (not Series B-D growth stage), and role emphasizes advertising monetization tradeoffs (fringe of 'ads-adjacent' domain avoidance). AI/ML relevance present but secondary to quantitative modeling. Best fit if candidate can embrace mature-company product complexity and engagement-driven (not ads-first) focus.

roles.title: Director of Machine Learning, Safety & Mods
roles.fit_notes: Strong technical and seniority alignment—Director role with team leadership, LLM/ML expertise directly relevant, and safety/moderation systems leverage causal reasoning. Significant concerns: Reddit is late-stage/public (not Series B-D growth preference), safety/moderation work touches content moderation which can be ethically complex, and domain differs from candidate's consumer/marketplace/analytics background. Compensation upside exists but role demands significant org/political navigation.

custom_questions.label: Please provide the name of your current (or most recent) company
Describe the size and structure of the largest team you have managed:
Describe the most complex Machine Learning system you've successfully delivered from pilot to production at scale within the last five years.
Detail a project where you led the effort to adopt or integrate a Large Language Model (LLM) into a production system.

roles.title: Senior Data Scientist - Fraud Prevention
roles.fit_notes: Nextdoor is a strong domain fit (community/marketplace, not ads/defense/crypto) with a meaningful fraud prevention mission. The Senior DS role offers seniority and compensation alignment, and fraud detection involves some causal/experimental thinking. However, this is a mature public company (post-IPO) rather than growth-stage 0-to-1 building, and the fraud prevention focus is narrower than the candidate's core experimentation platform expertise. The role emphasizes detection/metrics/moderation over the hands-on 50/50 IC-to-strategic split preferred, and AI relevance is moderate (evaluating tool effectiveness vs. developing LLM products).

roles.title: Staff Data Scientist - AI-enabled Product Understanding (Inference)
roles.fit_notes: Exceptional craft and seniority alignment: this Staff role is tailor-made for deep causal inference expertise in marketplace search/discovery, with explicit 0-to-1 product ownership and player-coach partnership. Compensation ($241K base+ aligns perfectly with $230-260K target, and Airbnb's scale/prestige offer strong growth trajectory. Core concern: Airbnb is a mature public company (Series E+), not the Series B-D growth-stage preference—this is a "scaling at massive scale" role rather than early-stage building, which may feel less entrepreneurial than desired.

roles.title: Senior Data Scientist, Consumer
roles.fit_notes: Strong craft match—Reddit's emphasis on experimentation, causal analysis, and A/B testing infrastructure directly aligns with candidate's core expertise. Senior-level role with player-coach potential (leading experimentation practices, guiding teams). However, Reddit is post-Series D (public, mature) rather than growth-stage 0-to-1, and AI/ML product development is not a primary focus of this posting. Compensation ($177-363K range, likely $240-280K at senior level) fits target, and domain (consumer community platform) is a clear avoid-list miss given candidate's aversion to ads-tech—Reddit's dual revenue model (ads heavily featured in hiring messaging) creates meaningful misalignment.

custom_questions.label: Please provide the name of your current (or most recent) company

roles.title: Senior Data Science Manager, AI Products
roles.fit_notes: This is a strategically interesting but operationally misaligned opportunity. Dropbox's new AI-first pivot (Dash Experience) offers genuine 0-to-1 building and direct product impact at a scale that's rare, with strong domain fit (not ads/crypto/defense) and meaningful AI/ML responsibilities. However, the role is primarily a people manager (70% team leadership) rather than the 50/50 IC/strategic balance the candidate prefers, and compensation data is incomplete/outdated, making it difficult to assess whether Dropbox will stretch to $230-260K for this level. The 'startup team within a public company' framing is appealing but carries execution risk—Dropbox is a mature public company (not Series B-D), which may constrain the scrappy builder culture the candidate values.

roles.title: Senior Staff Data Scientist, Guest & Host Marketplace AI
roles.fit_notes: Excellent craft and seniority alignment: this Senior Staff role is purpose-built around causal inference, A/B testing, and user behavior analysis in a marketplace—the candidate's core strength. Compensation ($241K+ base, likely $280-380K total comp at this level) exceeds target. Strong culture fit with hands-on technical leadership, product collaboration, and direct impact on guest experience. Primary concern: Airbnb is post-Series D, a mature public company (~$300B market cap), not the Series B-D growth-stage startup the candidate prefers. Secondary: less explicit AI/ML product development than implied; focus is analytics/personalization rather than building new LLM capabilities.

roles.title: Senior Marketing Analyst (Multiple Levels)
roles.location: Canada - Remote (ON, AB, BC, or NS Only)
roles.fit_notes: Instacart is a strong domain fit (consumer marketplace, no blockers), and the compensation trajectory aligns well with candidate's $230-260K target given levels.fyi data. However, this is a **marketing analytics role, not an experimentation/causal inference platform role**—a significant craft mismatch for someone whose core expertise and passion is building experimentation infrastructure. The role is also IC-heavy analytics (not player-coach/Staff-level), lacks AI/ML product development, and sits in a mature Series D company, not the 0-to-1 growth-stage candidate prefers. It's a solid stepping stone but doesn't leverage or grow the candidate's core superpower.

roles.title: Senior Data Scientist
roles.fit_notes: Dropbox offers solid craft match (experimentation, analytics, user behavior analysis) and strong domain fit (consumer productivity, not ads/crypto), with meaningful AI integration work in core product. However, this is a maturity-stage public company (Series G/IPO), not the growth-stage 0-to-1 builder environment the candidate targets. Compensation appears misreported but likely $200-240K range—below target. The role leans toward analytical partnership rather than player-coach IC leadership with hands-on technical depth.

roles.title: Principal Data Scientist, Ads
roles.fit_notes: Strong technical and seniority alignment—Principal role with deep causal inference, econometrics, and A/B testing leadership is exactly the candidate's expertise. However, this is fundamentally an ads-tech role, which is explicitly on the candidate's avoid list. Reddit is also past growth-stage (public, mature ads business) rather than the preferred Series B-D 0-to-1 environment. Compensation is achievable but not a major draw given domain misalignment.

custom_questions.label: Please provide the name of your current (or most recent) company

roles.title: Sr. Principal Scientist, Secure Work Enablement
roles.fit_notes: Strong misalignment despite perfect seniority level. AWS/SWE is a 650+ person org within legacy enterprise (Amazon), not a growth-stage builder environment. Core expertise in experimentation platforms and causal inference has minimal relevance to LLM/GenAI research focus. While AI/ML relevance is high, the role is pure research scientist (not product-focused), lacks hands-on experimentation infrastructure work, and operates within a massive corporate bureaucracy antithetical to the candidate's stated preference for 0-to-1 building and direct product impact.
roles.description:
**DESCRIPTION**
---------------


At Secure Work Enablement (SWE), we're pioneering breakthrough AI technologies that are fundamentally transforming how millions of teams and businesses work. Our mission combines leading machine learning research with Amazon's unparalleled expertise in enterprise computing and security to create the next generation of intelligent workplace solutions.  

  

Our portfolio encompasses four innovative domains where applied AI research is critical: Next\-Generation End User Computing, where we're developing novel machine learning models for human\-AI collaboration; Amazon One's advanced biometric systems; Secure Collaboration platforms Wickr and Chime; and Gaia – our revolutionary AI\-native workspace that's redefining human\-AI agent interactions. Each area presents unique opportunities for advancing the state\-of\-the\-art in machine learning, natural language processing, and AI systems.  

  

We're at an inflection point where traditional workplace computing is being revolutionized by AI technologies. Our distinctive challenge lies in solving complex machine learning problems at scale while maintaining strict security requirements – from developing sophisticated ML models for secure information access to creating intelligent systems that can understand and enhance human productivity in real\-time.  

  

With an accomplished team of 650\+ technologists and multiple tier\-1 services, we're seeking a Senior Principal Scientist to spearhead our AI research and development initiatives. This role offers the opportunity to tackle unprecedented challenges in applied machine learning, including:  

  

* Developing novel AI architectures for secure, enterprise\-grade collaborative systems
* Advancing the science of human\-AI interaction in workplace environments
* Creating new frameworks for AI agent orchestration and optimization
* Pioneering new approaches to privacy\-preserving machine learning

  

Your scientific leadership will be crucial in defining and solving complex AI problems that will shape the future of work for years to come, working across multiple research teams and beyond SWE's boundaries.  

  

Key job responsibilities  

As a Senior Principal Scientist in Secure Work Enablement (SWE), you will have deep subject matter expertise in the area of large language models and generative AI across various modalities. You will work with multiple teams of scientists and engineers to translate business and functional requirements into concrete deliverables. You will invent new product experiences that enable teams and agents to collaborate effectively. You will have the opportunity to invent new approaches that help our customers achieve better results using natural language as the interface. Your inputs will shape the future of work.  

You will liaise with internal Amazon partners and work on bringing state\-of\-the\-art LLM/GenAI models to production. You will stay abreast of the latest developments in the field of GenAI and identify opportunities to improve the efficiency and productivity of the team. You will define a long\-term science vision for our business, driven by our customer’s needs, and translate it into actionable plans for our team of applied scientists, and engineers. Finally, you will work with academic partners to support our in\-house talent with direct access to leading research and mentoring.**BASIC QUALIFICATIONS**
------------------------

* Graduate degree in Computer science/Math or related field.
* Experience in building complex, real\-time systems involving Agentic AI, Personalization and Reinforced Learning with successful delivery to customers. Demonstrated track record of project delivery for large, cross\-functional projects with evolving requirements.
* Computer Science fundamentals in data structures, algorithm design and complexity analysis.
* Ability to develop machine learning platform strategy in the domain of recommender systems.
* Demonstrated track record of peer\-reviewed scientific publications that advance state\-of\-the art for applied science.

**PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS**
----------------------------

* 15\+ years of relevant, broad research experience after PhD degree or equivalent.
* Ability to take a project from requirements gathering and design to actual product launch
* Exceptional customer understanding skills including the ability to discover the true challenges to efficient product discovery, and experience in leading science efforts to meet timelines with optimal solutions. Deep expertise in Machine Learning as applied to large\-scale generative models Proficiency in programming for algorithm and code reviews.
* Strong core competency in mathematics and statistics.
* Track record of successful projects in algorithm design and product development.
* Publications at top\-tier peer\-reviewed conferences or journals.
* Strong prior experience with mentorship and/or management of senior scientists and engineers.
